title: How to Reset Your Active Directory / SSO Password
text:
Q: How do I reset my Active Directory / SSO password?
A: To reset your Active Directory / SSO password, follow the steps below based on your operating system. 

Windows: 
1. Press 'Ctrl + Alt + Delete' on your keyboard. 
2. Click on 'Change a password'. 
3. Enter your current password in the first field. 
4. Enter your new password in the second field. 
5. Confirm your new password in the third field. 
6. Click 'OK' to finalize the password change. 
7. You will receive a confirmation message if the reset was successful. 

macOS: 
1. Click on the Apple menu in the top-left corner of your screen. 
2. Select 'System Preferences'. 
3. Click on 'Users & Groups'. 
4. Click on your user account and then click 'Reset Password...'. 
5. Enter your current password when prompted. 
6. Enter your new password and confirm it in the respective fields. 
7. Click 'Change Password' to complete the reset. 
8. A confirmation message will appear if the reset was successful. 

If you encounter any issues during the process, please contact the IT Helpdesk for assistance.
